result.stdout:
Usage: [command] [-h|--help] [--version]

Commands:
  info    Displays detailed DBF file metadata and structure information.
  read    Reads and displays DBF file contents.

commands.payload:
Usage: [command] [-h|--help] [--version]

Commands:
  info    Displays detailed DBF file metadata and structure information.
  read    Reads and displays DBF file contents.

result.stdout:
Usage: info [arguments...] [options...] [-h|--help] [--version]

Displays detailed DBF file metadata and structure information.

Arguments:
  [0] <string?>    Path to the DBF file to analyze, S3 URI (s3://bucket/key), or omit to read from stdin.

Options:
  --fields <bool>                 Show field definitions table. (Default: true)
  --header <bool>                 Show header information table. (Default: true)
  --stats <bool>                  Show record statistics table. (Default: true)
  --memo <bool>                   Show memo file information. (Default: true)
  -v|--verbose                    Show additional detailed information, including sample data. (Optional)
  -q|--quiet                      Suppress all informational output except for errors. (Optional)
  --encoding <string?>            Override character encoding (e.g., "UTF-8", "Windows-1252"). (Default: null)
  --ignore-missing-memo <bool>    Don't fail if the memo file is missing. (Default: true)

commands.payload:
Usage: info [arguments...] [options...] [-h|--help] [--version]

Displays detailed DBF file metadata and structure information.

Arguments:
  [0] <string?>    Path to the DBF file to analyze, S3 URI (s3://bucket/key), or omit to read from stdin.

Options:
  --fields <bool>                 Show field definitions table. (Default: true)
  --header <bool>                 Show header information table. (Default: true)
  --stats <bool>                  Show record statistics table. (Default: true)
  --memo <bool>                   Show memo file information. (Default: true)
  -v|--verbose                    Show additional detailed information, including sample data. (Optional)

result.stdout:
Usage: read [arguments...] [options...] [-h|--help] [--version]

Reads and displays DBF file contents.

Arguments:
  [0] <string?>    Path to the DBF file to read or omit to read from stdin.

Options:
  -f|--format <OutputFormat>      The output format (table, csv, tsv, json). (Default: Table)
  -o|--output <string?>           The output file path (default: stdout). (Default: null)
  -l|--limit <int?>               Maximum number of records to display. (Default: null)
  -s|--skip <int>                 Number of records to skip before reading. (Default: 0)
  --show-deleted                  Includes records marked as deleted in the output. (Optional)
  --fields <string?>              A comma-separated list of fields to include (e.g., "NAME,AGE"). (Default: null)
  -v|--verbose                    Enables verbose output, including file information. (Optional)
  -q|--quiet                      Suppresses all informational output except for data and errors. (Optional)
  --encoding <string?>            Overrides the character encoding for reading text fields (e.g., "UTF-8", "Windows-1252"). (Default: null)
  --ignore-case <bool>            Treats field names as case-insensitive. (Default: true)
  --trim-strings <bool>           Trims leading and trailing whitespace from string fields. (Default: true)
  --ignore-missing-memo <bool>    Prevents failure if a required memo file (.dbt) is missing. (Default: true)
  --bounding-box <string?>        Filter by bounding box "minX,minY,maxX,maxY" (shapefile only). (Default: null)
  --contains-point <string?>      Filter geometries containing point "x,y" (shapefile only). (Default: null)
  --intersects-with <string?>     Filter geometries intersecting with bounding box "minX,minY,maxX,maxY" (shapefile only). (Default: null)
  --nearest-point <string?>       Find features nearest to point "x,y" (shapefile only). (Default: null)
  --nearest-count <int?>          Number of nearest features to return (default: 1). (Default: null)
  --nearest-distance <double?>    Maximum distance for nearest neighbor search. (Default: null)
  --build-spatial-index           Build spatial index for faster queries (recommended for large files). (Optional)

commands.payload:
Usage: read [arguments...] [options...] [-h|--help] [--version]

Reads and displays DBF file contents.

Arguments:
  [0] <string?>    Path to the DBF file to read or omit to read from stdin.

Options:
  -f|--format <OutputFormat>      The output format (table, csv, tsv, json). (Default: Table)
  -o|--output <string?>           The output file path (default: stdout). (Default: null)
  -l|--limit <int?>               Maximum number of records to display. (Default: null)
  -s|--skip <int>                 Number of records to skip before reading. (Default: 0)
  --show-deleted                  Includes records marked as deleted in the output. (Optional)
  --fields <string?>              A comma-separated list of fields to include (e.g., "NAME,AGE"). (Default: null)
  -v|--verbose                    Enables verbose output, including file information. (Optional)
  -q|--quiet                      Suppresses all informational output except for data and errors. (Optional)
  --encoding <string?>            Overrides the character encoding for reading text fields (e.g., "UTF-8", "Windows-1252"). (Default: null)
  --ignore-case <bool>            Treats field names as case-insensitive. (Default: true)
  --trim-strings <bool>           Trims leading and trailing whitespace from string fields. (Default: true)
  --ignore-missing-memo <bool>    Prevents failure if a required memo file (.dbt) is missing. (Default: true)
  --bounding-box <string?>        Filter by bounding box "minX,minY,maxX,maxY" (shapefile only). (Default: null)
  --contains-point <string?>      Filter geometries containing point "x,y" (shapefile only). (Default: null)
  --intersects-with <string?>     Filter geometries intersecting with bounding box "minX,minY,maxX,maxY" (shapefile only). (Default: null)
  --nearest-point <string?>       Find features nearest to point "x,y" (shapefile only). (Default: null)
  --nearest-count <int?>          Number of nearest features to return (default: 1). (Default: null)
  --nearest-distance <double?>    Maximum distance for nearest neighbor search. (Default: null)
  --build-spatial-index           Build spatial index for faster queries (recommended for large files). (Optional)
